Items.Title: 好的、坏的和有偏见的：生成 AI 中的偏见是缺陷还是特征？ | The Good, the Bad, and the Biased: Is Bias in Generative AI a Flaw or a Feature?
Items.Description:
问题：您认为生成式 AI 中基于文化、种族、地理或性别的偏见是一个特征还是一个缺陷？

最近，这个问题一直在我脑海中盘旋。

最近，我问了 ChatGPT 一个相当无伤大雅的问题：

你能生成 10 个随机名称吗？

正如预期的那样，这对 ChatGPT 来说是一项简单的任务，我很高兴看到生成的名字具有相当多的多样性，并且在人眼看来，就像真正的人名一样。

然后我问了 ChatGPT：

你能用上面 10 个名字生成一个表格，每个名字有一个随机的位置、年龄和最喜欢的食物吗？

很快，ChatGPT 就创建了一个表格，其中包含我要求的准确信息。

但当我更深入地检查桌子时，有件事让我震惊……

当我反思我所观察到的情况时，我越来越清楚生成式 AI 解释提示的方式将对开发人员（以及其他所有人）将来如何利用这项技术产生强大的影响。

仔细看看

经过仔细检查，生成的随机名称、生成的随机位置和随机生成的最喜欢的食物并不像我最初想象的那样“随机”。

每个名字、位置和最喜欢的食物（在某种程度上）对应于一个人可能对拥有该名字、可能住在那个地方或将那个项目指定为他们最喜欢的食物的人的“文化期望”。

比如来自首尔的Aaron Kim，韩国最喜欢的食物是石锅拌饭。

我的第一个想法是，“哇！ 然后我的第二个想法是“等等！？ 然后我突然明白了； 在默认状态下，ChatGPT 在考虑我的问题、想法、陈述或提示时会带有某种程度的固有偏见。

有偏见的人工智能确实是人工智能

对于许多人来说，这不是一个革命性的想法，也不是什么让他们感到惊讶的事情。

如果有的话，这已经成为一个众所周知的问题，开发人员和工程师在为全球观众创造新技术时必须解决这个问题。

为什么这种特殊的偏见对我来说很突出，因为 ChatGPT 出于自己的“意志”，确定回答我的问题的最佳方式是将文化和地理偏见嵌入其答案中。

在某些方面，我为 ChatGPT 巧妙地嵌入这些类型的偏见而鼓掌，因为这是一件非常人性化的事情，但这是我们作为用户在使用 ChatGPT 或任何其他生成式 AI 技术时需要继续认识到的事情。

很容易将生成式 AI 视为一台使用人类语言模式给出最合乎逻辑的答案的计算机。 但是生成式人工智能和我们一样容易产生偏见，因为在很多方面它都是根据我们自己的偏见进行训练的。

用于训练生成式人工智能以使其信息丰富、有用且“像人类”的数据从根本上概括了我们人类的倾向和偏见。

但问题是，既然我们知道生成式 AI 系统可能有一些内在的偏见，我们该如何处理它们。

我们现在用生成式人工智能做什么？

这是一个有点复杂的问题，但一个答案可以是确保我们的问题或我们的提示足够具体，以帮助引导我们的 AI 伙伴实现我们期望的输出，尽管存在偏见。

因此，让我们以我之前分享的提示为例，让我们要求 ChatGPT 考虑它无意（或有意）融入其答案的偏见。

让我们试试这个：

你能否在不对文化、国籍、种族或性别产生偏见的情况下，再次随机分配表格中最喜欢的食物？

乍一看，这个问题似乎非常具体并且有点“领先”，但这正是重点。

作为 ChatGPT 的用户，我们的工作是在我们的提示和请求中明确说明。

考虑我之前的问题：

你能否生成一个包含以上 10 个名称的表，每个名称都有一个随机位置。 年龄，最喜欢的食物？

当要求一些“随机”的东西时，我对 ChatGPT 的期望是，ChatGPT 会为之前的随机名称选择随机位置、年龄和食物，而不考虑它之前生成的名称。

之所以得出这个结论，可能是因为在过去被问及类似问题时，它在与这些偏见保持一致时收到了积极的反馈。

并且明白， ChatGPT 没有做错任何事，它只是做了它设计的事情。 它利用过去的经验来预测最有可能满足我需求的预期结果。

那么我之前提示的澄清结果是什么：

你能否在不对文化、国籍、种族或性别产生偏见的情况下，再次随机分配表格中最喜欢的食物？

上面的结果更多地反映了我最初对我请求的属性的“随机性”的期望。

当 ChatGPT 得到更集中的指导时，它能够给我更容易满足我期望的结果。

比如来自首尔的Aaron Kim，现在韩国最喜欢的食物是烤蔬菜。

用你的措辞来管理偏见

因此，再花点时间考虑一下为什么“随机”一词可能不足以获得我预期或想要的结果。

考虑这一点的一种方法是考虑 ChatGPT 如何解释以下单词/短语：

  • 最好的
  • 最差
  • 最有效率
  • 最快的
  • 好的
  • 坏的
  • 最强
  • 最具影响力

虽然很难准确预测 ChatGPT 将如何解释这些词，但可以公平地假设它可能会像我们人类那样解释上述词。 它会对我们所说的那些特定单词或短语的含义做出一些假设，并会尽最大努力给出反映这些假设的结果。

花点时间考虑一下是什么让任何一件事成为“最好的”。

如果我现在问你，“你所在地区最好的海鲜是什么？”，你会如何确定这个问题的答案？

说到“最好”，你可能会考虑海鲜的价格、购买海鲜的地点、海鲜的新鲜度，甚至你可能会考虑你对不同种类海鲜的个人体验。你的地区。 然后，在考虑了这些信息之后，您可能会给出一些属于“最佳”的海鲜选项，或者只是一个对您来说是“最佳”的地方。

那么让我们看看 ChatGPT 如何回答类似的问题：

路易斯安那州巴吞鲁日最好的海鲜是什么？

注意：我选择路易斯安那州的巴吞鲁日是因为我在那里住了一段时间，而路易斯安那州总体上以其在美国的整体优质海鲜产品而闻名。

如果您以前使用过 ChatGPT，您应该不会对这种类型的响应感到惊讶。

但我的问题是“ChatGPT 如何确定什么是‘最好的’海鲜”？

好吧，让我们问一下 ChatGPT：

您如何确定路易斯安那州巴吞鲁日最好的海鲜是什么？

用它自己的话说，ChatGPT 表示它没有自己的偏好，但它使用经过训练的数据来确定“最佳”的含义。 它更多地解释了如何提出这些建议以及“最佳”如何因人而异。

正如我之前提到的，ChatGPT 对“最好”的偏见本质上并不是坏事，但如果在使用 ChatGPT 时您不知道它如何使用其偏见来回答或回答问题，您可能不会意识到您的回答给出的不是“客观事实”。

但这与我作为开发人员有什么关系？

那么这如何适用于您作为开发人员或工程师呢？

简而言之，了解您正在使用的生成式 AI 系统的偏见并避免迫使 AI 系统过度解释您的意图的术语，将在您使用生成式 AI 工具时提供极大帮助。

很容易假设向 ChatGPT 寻求有关如何构建代码的帮助不会受到偏见的严重影响，但考虑到代码是由人类和人类代码以直接影响其技术方法的方式编写的。

编码/编程与其说是一门科学，不如说是一门艺术，了解偏见会影响我们作为开发人员编写和考虑代码的方式，这一点很重要。

因此，这里有一些快速提示可以帮助您进行提示：

人工智能系统如何理解这些单词或短语对您来说似乎很明显，但如果单词的含义过于模糊或过于偏颇，您可能会得到意想不到的结果。

即使有看似精心设计的问题或提示，人工智能系统也可能仍在使用某种形式的偏见或推理来提供高质量的回答。 在获得看似合理的响应后，尝试了解系统如何以及为何得出该特定结论的背景信息。

注意：没有办法 100% 确定生成式 AI 系统给你的关于它为什么以某种方式回答提示的反应是完全准确的。 例如，人工智能系统可能会回答你的问题，但不是用真相，而是用它假设你想知道的东西。 请注意这一点。

仅仅因为您要求 ChatGPT 以特定语言生成具有针对特定目的的特定要求的代码，并不意味着您得到了完全客观、公正的响应。 从根本上说，生成式人工智能是基于过去积累的数据，它的反应反映了这一点。 请注意，它现在生成的代码基于过去生成的代码，可能反映出可能不会对您当前的工作产生积极影响的技术和编码偏差。 过去处理技术问题的范例可能不是未来进步所需的方向。

汇集一切

简而言之，生成式 AI 在回答特定提示或问题时可能存在的偏见本身并不是坏事，甚至不是缺陷。 如果有的话，因为这些系统是由人类带着自己的偏见创造的，这是可以预料的。

作为这些技术的好管家，我们有责任在使用它们时理解它们绝不是完全客观的，并且从根本上必须消除某种形式的偏见才能达到最佳效果。

在使用这些技术时，请继续记住问问自己系统是如何以及为什么得出结论的。 此外，我们应该始终致力于在我们的提示中尽可能具体和直接，以帮助指导我们的 AI 同事做出最佳响应并最大程度地减少混淆。

在一个从根本上由我们的个人观点和偏见塑造的世界中，很自然地假设我们设计和创造的东西会带有这些偏见。

如果我们继续直面这些偏见并诚实地了解它们如何影响我们的工作，特别是在这些新兴的生成人工智能领域，我们将继续拥有光明的未来，在那里我们可以完成我们从未想过的事情。

你对生成式人工智能的偏见有何看法？ 请在评论中分享它们！

一切顺利，

布拉德斯顿亨利

原文:

Question: Would you consider cultural, racial, geographical, or gender based-biases in generative AI a feature or a flaw?

As of late, this question has been swirling around my mind.

Recently, I asked ChatGPT a fairly innocuous question:

Can you generate 10 random names?

As expected, this was an easy task for ChatGPT and I was pleased to see that the names generated had a decent amount of variety and, to the human eye, read as real human names.

I then asked ChatGPT:

Can you generate a table with the above 10 names with each name having a random location, age, and favorite food?

And in short order, ChatGPT created a table with the exact information I had requested.

But as I inspected the table more deeply, something struck me as...

...odd.

And as I reflected on what I observed, it became clear to me that how generative AI interprets prompts will have a powerful impact on how developers (and everyone else) will utilize this technology in the future.

Taking a Closer Look

Upon closer inspection, the random names generated, the random locations generated AND the random generated favorite foods weren’t as "random" as I first thought.

Each name, location, and favorite food corresponded (to some degree) to a “cultural expectation” one might have for a person who would have that given name, who might live in that location or would designate that item as their favorite food.

For example, Aaron Kim from Seoul, South Korea’s favorite food was bibimbap.

My first thought was, “Wow! That’s pretty cool that ChatGPT tried to match random names along geographical and cultural lines.”

And then my second thought was “Wait!? Did ChatGPT just randomize names along geographical and cultural lines, without me asking?”

And then it dawned on me; In its default state, ChatGPT operates with some level of inherent bias when considering my questions, thoughts, statements, or prompts.

An AI with Bias is an AI Indeed

For many, this isn’t a revolutionary thought or something that even slightly surprises them.

If anything, this has become a very well known issue that developers and engineers have had to combat when creating new technology for a global audience.

Why this particular instance of bias stood out to me was that ChatGPT, of its own "volition", determined the best way to answer my query was to embed cultural and geographical bias into its answer.

In some ways, I applaud ChatGPT for its cleverness to embed those types of biases, as that is a very human thing to do, but it’s something we as users need to continue to be cognizant of when using ChatGPT or any other generative AI technology.

It can be easy to view Generative AI as just a computer giving the most logical answer using human speech patterns. But generative AI is just as prone to biases as we are, because in many ways it was trained on our own biases.

The data used to train a generative AI to make it so informative, helpful and “human-like” fundamentally encapsulate our human tendencies and biases.

But the question becomes, what do we do with Generative AI systems now that we know they may have some biases built-in.

What Do We Do with Generative AI Now?

This is a somewhat complex question but one answer to this can be to ensure our questions, or our prompts, are specific enough to help guide our AI companions toward our desired output, despite the bias.

So let’s take, for example, the prompt I shared earlier and let’s ask ChatGPT to consider the biases that it unintentionally (or intentionally) baked into its answer.

Let’s try this:

Could you randomize the favorite foods in the table again without biasing the randomness to culture, nationality, race or gender?

At first glance, this question seems hyper specific and somewhat “leading” but that’s exactly the point.

As the user of ChatGPT, it is our job to be explicit in our prompts and requests.

Consider my previous question:

Can you generate a table with the above 10 names with each name having a random location. age, and favorite food?

My expectation from ChatGPT when asking for something “random” was for ChatGPT to select random locations, ages, and foods for the previous randomized names with no consideration for the names it had previously generated.

What ChatGPT interpreted from my prompt was that I wanted those random items to be contextualized to the names and cultural leanings those names represent.

It may have come to that conclusion because when asked similar questions in the past, it received positive feedback when aligning with those biases.

And understand, ChatGPT didn’t do anything wrong, it just did what it was designed to do. It used its past experiences to predict the desired outcome that would most likely fit my needs.

So what was the outcome from the clarification of my previous prompt:

Could you randomize the favorite foods in the table again without biasing the randomness to culture, nationality, race or gender?

The above outcome more reflects my original expectation of “randomness” for my requested attributes.

When ChatGPT was given more focused direction, it was able to give me results that more readily satisfied what I desired.

For Example, Aaron Kim from Seoul, South Korea’s favorite food is now roasted vegetables.

Managing Bias Using Your Wording

So take another moment to consider why the word “random” may have not been sufficient to get the results I expected or wanted.

One way to consider this is to think about how ChatGPT interprets the words/phrases below:

  • Best
  • Worst
  • Most Efficient
  • Fastest
  • Good
  • Bad
  • Strongest
  • Most Influential

Though it's hard to exactly predict how ChatGPT would interpret these words, it's fair to assume it would likely interpret the above words similar to how we as humans would. It would make some assumptions on what we meant by those particular words or phrases and would try its absolute best to give results that reflect those assumptions.

Take a moment to consider what makes any one thing the “best”.

If I asked you right now, “What is the best Seafood in your area?”, how would you determine the answer to that question?

When it comes to “best” you might consider the price of the seafood, the location of where the seafood could be purchased, the freshness of the seafood, and you might even consider your personal experience with the different types of seafood you have had in your area. Then, after considering that information, you would likely give a few seafood options that rank amongst the "best" or just one place that stands out to you as the “best”.

So let’s see how ChatGPT answers a similar question:

What is the best Seafood in Baton Rouge, Louisiana?

NOTE: I chose Baton Rouge, Louisiana because I lived there for a time and Louisiana, in general, is known for its overall quality seafood offerings in the US.

If you have used ChatGPT before, you shouldn't be surprised by this type of response.

But my question to you is “How Did ChatGPT determine what is the ‘best’ seafood”?

Well, let's ask ChatGPT:

How did you determine what was the best seafood in Baton Rouge, Louisiana?

In its own words, ChatGPT expresses that it doesn’t have its own preferences, but it uses its trained data to determine what “best” means. It explains a bit more about how it came about making those recommendations and how "best" may differ from person to person.

This bias that ChatGPT has toward what is “best” is not inherently bad, as I mentioned earlier, but if while using ChatGPT you are not aware of how it uses its bias to respond to or answers questions, you may not realize the response you were given is not the "objective truth".

But What Does This Have to do with Me as a Developer?

So how does this apply to you as a developer or engineer?

In plain and simple terms, understanding the biases of the generative AI system you are using and avoiding terms that force the AI system to overly interpret your intent, will help you immensely when using generative AI tools.

It's easy to assume that asking ChatGPT for assistance on how to build code won't be heavily impacted by bias but consider that code is written by humans and humans code in ways that directly impact their technical approach.

Coding/programming is less of a science and more of an art, and understanding that bias has an impact on how we as developers write and consider our code is important to understand.

So here are a few quick tips to help with your prompting:

1) Do not use words and phrases like “best”, “worst”, or “most XYZ” without understanding how the generative AI is interpreting those words

It may seem obvious to you how the AI system may be understanding those words or phrases but if the meaning of a word is too vague or overly biased, you may get results you didn’t expect.

2) Ask the Generative AI system to explain why it gave you that response.

Even with seemingly very well-crafted questions or prompts, the AI system is likely still using some form of bias or inference to provide a quality response. After getting a response that seems reasonable, try and get some context on how and why the system came to that particular conclusion.

NOTE: There is no way to be 100% sure that the response a Generative AI system gives you about why it answered a prompt in a certain way is completely accurate. The AI system may, for example, answer your question, not with the truth, but with what it assumes you would like to know. Please be aware of this.

3) Do NOT assume prompting for technical or coding responses makes you immune to bias

Just because you are asking ChatGPT to generate code in a specific language with specific requirements for a specific purpose does NOT mean you are getting a completely objective unbiased response. Fundamentally, generative AI is based on data accumulated from the past and its responses reflect that. Be aware that the code it generates now, is based on code generated in the past and may reflect technical and coding biases that may not positively impact your current work. Past paradigms of approaching a technical problem may not be the direction needed for future progress.

Bringing it all Together

In short, the biases that Generative AI may have when answering particular prompts or questions is not inherently bad, or even a flaw. If anything, because these systems are created by humans with their own biases, this is to be expected.

It is our responsibility, as good stewards of these technologies, to use them with the understanding that they are in no way fully objective and fundamentally must work off of some form of bias to be optimally effective.

When using these technologies, continue to remember to ask yourself how and why a system came to its conclusion. Also, we should always aim to be as specific and direct in our prompts to help guide our AI associates to the best possible response and to minimize confusion.

In a world that is fundamentally shaped by our individual perspectives and biases, it is only natural to assume the things we design and create carry those biases along with them.

If we continue to confront those biases head on and are honest about how they impact what we do, specifically in these emerging spaces of generative AI, we will continue to have a bright future where we accomplish things, we never thought possible.

What are your thoughts about the bias in Generative AI? Please share them in the comments!

All the best,

Bradston Henry

Items.Title: Rust 中的服务器端渲染——一个 Dall.E 用例 | Server-side rendering in Rust - a Dall.E use-case
Items.Description:
上周，我决定去看看 OpenAI 的图像生成能力。 但是，我注意到必须付费才能使用Web 界面，而API是免费的，尽管有速率限制。 到目前为止，我已经创建了一个REST API 。 在这篇文章中，我想描述如何创建具有服务器端渲染的 Web 应用程序。

上下文

我已经将 Axum 用于之前的 REST API，所以我决定继续。

服务器端渲染 webapp 类似于 REST API。 唯一的区别是前者返回 HTML 页面，而后者返回 JSON 有效负载。 从架构的角度来看，没有区别； 但是，从开发的角度来看，它起着巨大的作用。

你得到一个结构； 你序列化它，你就完成了。 您甚至可以手动完成； 没什么大不了的-尽管有点无聊。 另一方面，HTML 需要对标签进行精确排序：如果您手动创建它，维护将是一场噩梦。 我们发明了模板来生成带有代码的顺序敏感代码。

虽然模板可能由来已久，但 PHP 是使它普及的语言。 编写常规 HTML，并在必要时添加需要动态解释的片段。 在 JVM 世界中，我使用 JSP 和 Apache Velocity（后者）来生成 RTF 文档。

正如我上面提到的，我想继续使用 Axum。 这是我为 Rust 找到的模板库的一小部分示例：

然而，作为一名开发人员，我本质上是懒惰的，我想要开箱即用的与 Axum 集成的东西。 快速的 Google 搜索将我带到axum-template ，它看起来很新但非常有活力。 该库是对 handlebars、askama 和 minijinja 的抽象。 您可以随时使用 API 和更改实现。

设置 axum-template 相对简单。 首先，我们添加对 Cargo 的依赖：

然后，我们根据底层实现创建一个引擎，并配置 Axum 来使用它：在这里，我使用的是 Jinja：

 type AppEngine = Engine<Environment<'static>>; //1 #[derive(Clone, FromRef)] struct AppState { //2 engine: AppEngine, } #[tokio::main] async fn main() { let mut jinja = Environment::new(); //3 jinja.set_source(Source::from_path("templates")); //4 let app = Router::new() .route("/", get(home)) .with_state(AppState { //5 engine: Engine::from(jinja), }); }
  1. 创建类型别名
  2. 创建一个专用结构来保存引擎状态
  3. 创建特定于 Jinja 的环境
  4. 配置文件夹以从中读取模板。 该路径是相对于您启动二进制文件的位置； 它不应该是src文件夹的一部分。 我花了很多时间来实现它。
  5. 配置 Axum 以使用引擎

以下是基本项目：

  • 模板存储在类似哈希表的结构中。 使用 MiniJinja 实现，根据上面的配置， Key只是文件名，例如home.html
  • 最后的S参数没有要求。 该库将读取其属性并使用它们来填充模板。

我不会详细介绍模板本身，因为文档非常好。

隐含回报

它与模板无关，但这个迷你项目让我思考了impl返回类型。 在我之前的 REST 项目中，我注意到 Axum 处理程序函数返回impl ，但我没有考虑它。 这确实很简单：

如果您的函数返回一个实现MyTrait类型，您可以将其返回类型写为-> impl MyTrait 。 这可以大大简化您的类型签名！

然而，它有有趣的后果。 如果您返回单一类型，它就像一个魅力。 但是，如果您返回多个，则您要么需要所有返回类型的共同特征，要么对其进行明确说明。

这是原始样本：

 async fn call(engine: AppEngine, Form(state): Form<InitialPageState>) -> impl IntoResponse { RenderHtml(Key("home.html".to_owned()), engine, state) }

如果页面状态需要区分成功和错误，我们必须创建两个专用结构。

 async fn call(engine: AppEngine, Form(state): Form<InitialPageState>) -> Response { //1 let page_state = PageState::from(state); if page_state.either.is_left() { RenderHtml(Key("home.html".to_owned()), engine, page_state.either.left().unwrap()).into_response() //2 } else { RenderHtml(Key("home.html".to_owned()), engine, page_state.either.right().unwrap()).into_response() //2 } }
  1. 不能使用impl IntoResponse ； 需要使用明确的Response类型
  2. 将返回值显式转换为Response

使用应用程序

您可以从源代码构建或运行DockerHub上提供的 Docker 映像。 唯一的要求是通过环境变量提供 OpenAI 身份验证令牌：

 docker run -it --rm -p 3000:3000 -e OPENAI_TOKEN=... nfrankel/rust-dalle:0.1.0

享受！

结论

这个小项目让我发现了 Rust 的另一面：使用 Axum 进行 HTML 模板化。 这不是 Rust 的常见用例，但无论如何它都是它的一部分。

在 Dall.E 方面，这些功能并没有给我留下特别深刻的印象。 也许我没有设法以正确的方式描述结果。 我需要提高我的即时工程技能。

无论如何，我很高兴我开发了这个界面，即使只是为了好玩。

这篇文章的完整源代码可以在 GitHub 上找到：

该项目是一个允许调用 OpenAI API 的外观。

您可以从源代码构建或使用 Docker 映像。

 docker run -it --rm -p 3000:3000 \ docker run -it --rm -p 3000:3000 \
           -e OPENAI_TOKEN=... \ -e OPENAI_TOKEN=... \
           更进一步：
  •  阿克苏姆
  • 迷你神社
  • 图像生成API
 最初于 2023 年 4 月 30日发表于A Java Geek 
 蒂姆·麦克纳马拉 (Tim McNamara) - 22 年 10 月 28 日
 亚伦·克里斯蒂安森 - 22 年 10 月 23 日

 如果 nfrankel 没有被暂停，他们仍然可以从他们的仪表板重新发布他们的帖子。
 笔记：
 感谢您维护 DEV 社区的安全。 以下是标记 nfrankel 的方法：
 降低 nfrankel 的所有帖子的可见度
报告其他不当行为
取消标记 nfrankel 将恢复其帖子的默认可见性。


原文:

Last week, I decided to see the capabilities of OpenAI's image generation. However, I noticed that one has to pay to use the web interface, while the API was free, even though rate-limited. Dall.E offers Node.js and Python samples, but I wanted to keep learning Rust. So far, I've created a REST API. In this post, I want to describe how you can create a webapp with server-side rendering.

The context

Tokio is a runtime for asynchronous programming for Rust; Axum is a web framework that leverages the former. I already used Axum for the previous REST API, so I decided to continue.

A server-side rendering webapp is similar to a REST API. The only difference is that the former returns HTML pages, and the latter JSON payloads. From an architectural point of view, there's no difference; from a development one, however, it plays a huge role.

There's no visual requirement in JSON, so ordering is not an issue. You get a struct; you serialize it, and you are done. You can even do it manually; it's no big deal - though a bit boring. On the other hand, HTML requires a precise ordering of the tags: if you create it manually, maintenance is going to be a nightmare. We invented templating to generate order-sensitive code with code.

While templating is probably age-old, PHP was the language to popularize it. One writes regular HTML and, when necessary, adds the snippets that need to be dynamically interpreted. In the JVM world, I used JSPs and Apache Velocity, the latter, to generate RTF documents.

Templating in Axum

As I mentioned above, I want to continue using Axum. Axum doesn't offer any templating solution out-of-the-box, but it allows integrating any solution through its API.

Here is a small sample of templating libraries that I found for Rust:

As a developer, however, I'm lazy by essence, and I wanted something integrated with Axum out of the box. A quick Google search lead me to axum-template, which seems pretty new but very dynamic. The library is an abstraction over handlebars, askama, and minijinja. You can use the API and change implementation whenever you want.

axum-template in short

Setting up axum-template is relatively straightforward. First, we add the dependency to Cargo:

Then, we create an engine depending on the underlying implementation and configure Axum to use it: Here, I'm using Jinja:

type AppEngine = Engine<Environment<'static>>; //1 #[derive(Clone, FromRef)]
struct AppState { //2 engine: AppEngine,
} #[tokio::main]
async fn main() { let mut jinja = Environment::new(); //3 jinja.set_source(Source::from_path("templates")); //4 let app = Router::new() .route("/", get(home)) .with_state(AppState { //5 engine: Engine::from(jinja), });
}
  1. Create a type alias
  2. Create a dedicated structure to hold the engine state
  3. Create a Jinja-specific environment
  4. Configure the folder to read templates from. The path is relative to the location where you start the binary; it shouldn't be part of the src folder. I spent a nontrivial amount of time to realize it.
  5. Configure Axum to use the engine

Here are the base items:

  • Engine is a facade over the templating library
  • Templates are stored in a hashtable-like structure. With the MiniJinja implementation, according to the configuration above, Key is simply the filename, e.g., home.html
  • The final S parameter has no requirement. The library will read its attributes and use them to fill the template.

I won't go into the details of the template itself, as the documentation is quite good.

The impl return

It has nothing to do with templating, but this mini-project allowed me to ponder the impl return type. In my previous REST project, I noticed that Axum handler functions return impl, but I didn't think about it. It's indeed pretty simple:

If your function returns a type that implements MyTrait, you can write its return type as -> impl MyTrait. This can help simplify your type signatures quite a lot!

-- Rust by example

However, it has interesting consequences. If you return a single type, it works like a charm. However, if you return more than one, you either need a common trait across all returned types or to be explicit about it.

Here's the original sample:

async fn call(engine: AppEngine, Form(state): Form<InitialPageState>) -> impl IntoResponse { RenderHtml(Key("home.html".to_owned()), engine, state)
}

If the page state needs to differentiate between success and error, we must create two dedicated structures.

async fn call(engine: AppEngine, Form(state): Form<InitialPageState>) -> Response { //1 let page_state = PageState::from(state); if page_state.either.is_left() { RenderHtml(Key("home.html".to_owned()), engine, page_state.either.left().unwrap()).into_response() //2 } else { RenderHtml(Key("home.html".to_owned()), engine, page_state.either.right().unwrap()).into_response() //2 }
}
  1. Cannot use impl IntoResponse; need to use the explicit Response type
  2. Explicit transform the return value to Response

Using the application

You can build from the source or run the Docker image, available at DockerHub. The only requirement is to provide an OpenAI authentication token via an environment variable:

docker run -it --rm -p 3000:3000 -e OPENAI_TOKEN=... nfrankel/rust-dalle:0.1.0

Enjoy!

Conclusion

This small project allowed me to discover another side of Rust: HTML templating with Axum. It's not the usual use case for Rust, but it's part of it anyway.

On the Dall.E side, I was not particularly impressed with the capabilities. Perhaps I didn't manage to describe the results in the right way. I'll need to up my prompt engineering skills.

In any case, I'm happy that I developed the interface, if only for fun.

The complete source code for this post can be found on GitHub:

The project is a facade that allows calling the OpenAI API.

You can build from source or use the Docker image.

docker run -it --rm -p 3000:3000 \
           -e OPENAI_TOKEN=... \
           nfrankel/rust-dalle:0.1.0



To go further:

  • Axum
  • axum-template
  • MiniJinja
  • Dall.E webapp
  • Image generation API

Max Gritchin - Oct 21 '22

Kingkor Roy Tirtho - Oct 20 '22

Items.Title: Hashnode 训练营第 2 天：个人品牌和社区参与 | Hashnode Bootcamp Day 2: Personal Branding and Community Engagement
Items.Description:
嘿！ 你听说过哈希节点训练营吗？ 这是一个从 5 月 1 日到 3 日为期三天的活动，它帮助像我们这样的开发人员提高了写作技巧，与其他专业人士建立联系，并在我们的职业生涯中取得成功。 即使您刚刚开始涉足科技领域，这个训练营也非常适合您！

如果你参加了，你会得到这么多的好处！ 您本可以在技术社区中发出自己的声音，结识新朋友以帮助您的职业生涯，并提高您的写作技巧。 另外，您也会激励其他开发人员尽力而为！

您本可以获得如此多的知识并建立对您将来有帮助的联系。 所以，不要错过个人和专业成长的机会！

他分享了他的非传统技术背景，作为一名自学成才的开发人员，他于 2013 年开始编码并于 2014 年创建社区活动。他开始分享他学到的所有东西，这促使他在 2015 年撰写了他的第一篇博文，获得了超过一百万读者，激励他继续创作内容。 他还于 2016 年开始参加会议，并于 2017 年创办了一家名为 React Native Training 的培训公司，这帮助他获得了在 AWS 工作三年的机会，然后在过去两年进入区块链领域。

个人品牌

纳德谈到了个人品牌的重要性，以及人们的不在场（当他不在场时）如何充分说明他们。 在一个人们非常忙碌且注意力有限的世界里，充分利用人们给你的时间是很重要的。 这意味着要清楚你想在 Twitter 和 LinkedIn 等平台上传播什么，这样你才能在多面手的海洋中脱颖而出。 清楚地描述你是谁或你想成为什么样的人很重要，而且支付专家来审查和推荐你个人在线状态的更新可能会有所帮助。
他强调在特定领域专业化的重要性，而不是与其他人混在一起。 他建议比其他人花更多的时间在特定的技术或技能上，从而真正擅长它，从而带来更多的机会和持续的优化。 重要的是假设每个人都有时间限制，并且要简洁明了并在工作申请或在线展示中脱颖而出。

在线存在/社交媒体

这可以带来机会和建立关系。 重要的是要始终如一、乐于助人而不期望任何回报，并且要真实。 真实可能会让人感到不舒服，但这对于建立真诚的关系很重要。 要在社交媒体上保持真实，您应该做自己，而不是专注于取悦其他人。 与你想要的领域中有影响力的人接触，并与他们积极互动。 在社交媒体上保持一致并在参与和噱头之间找到平衡非常重要。 使用吸引注意力的策略，但不要过分依赖点击诱饵。 此外，考虑在 Stack Overflow 上反向链接您的社交媒体资料。

要增加社交媒体的参与度，重要的是链接到所有其他社交媒体并使用视频和图像等富媒体。 做真实的自己，即使这意味着违背观众的意愿。 使用适当的间距和标题来格式化您的文章，并考虑与其他人合作以覆盖更多的受众。

内容创作

学习新事物并将其浓缩为可理解的信息，为有效教学和与他人分享提供了机会。 他解释了以不同格式重复使用内容和创建资源来帮助他人的好处。 他鼓励内容创作者不要被之前已经做过的事情吓倒，并记录他自己的问题解决方案。

纳德建议，如果其他人已经创建了一个项目或解决了一个问题，不要气馁，而是尝试改进它或添加他自己的独特风格。 他建议利用其他网络扩大规模并专注于一件事，而不是在许多事情上表现平平。 最后，他建议混合现有的想法来创造新的和创新的东西。 除了传统的赞助或广告之外，博主有哪些创造性的方式可以通过他的社交媒体形象获利？ 苏曼吉里

咨询是一种经常被忽视的通过技能获利的方式。 通过确定需要为其 DevRel 团队提供技术内容的公司，您可以按月提供服务并收取高额费用，例如每月 5,000 美元。 许多公司对这类服务都有大量预算，这对他们来说可能是一个很大的价值。 通过提供咨询服务，您可以赚更多的钱，做更少的工作，并与客户建立更好的关系。

广告收入值得增加吗？？ 因为它降低了博客的质量。请为其推荐一些替代品 Devarshi Shimpi

字节制造者。 印度

除非你是一家大公司，否则不值得添加广告，因为你从流量中获得的价值通常并不多。 相反，您可以作为顾问或承包商分包给寻求 DevRel 支持的公司，并在不展示广告的情况下赚取更多收入。 你仍然可以把一些内容放在你自己的博客上。

您使用哪些资源来有效管理社交媒体？ 我很难处理那个。
要在社交媒体上取得成效，重要的是选择几个平台来关注，而不是尝试做所有事情。 创建内容后，您可以复制它并在 LinkedIn 和 Twitter 等其他平台上共享。 关键是要专注于少数几个平台，而不是试图活跃在所有平台上。

您能否举例说明如何以新的方式呈现之前呈现的内容？ 尤格博拉纳

学校, 印度

开发人员通过将聊天 GPT 插件的 Python 代码转换为 JavaScript 创建了一个开源项目。 这导致了咨询机会，尽管由于忙碌而无法接受。 开发人员指出，有很多针对热门主题的教程，人们可以简单地表达自己对该主题的看法以获得关注。

如何写出好的内容，在写Ankita thakur thakur时要时刻牢记哪些要点

印度

创建好的内容包括以易于理解的方式教授复杂的主题，将信息浓缩成简洁的内容，并拥有吸引读者的高质量标题。 要做到这一点，应该选择一个自己感兴趣的话题，使内容对各个层次的听众都容易理解，把复杂的话题简单化，又不过分基础。 如果这些都完成了，那么内容就成功了。

对于那些不喜欢在公共场合学习以利用技术写作来吸引机会的人，请问您有什么建议。 我个人对公共学习感到焦虑，但我喜欢在社交媒体上分享技术内容 Stephany Ikebudu

尼日利亚

他相信每个人创造和分享内容也是在公开学习。 主要思想是将事物推向世界并使它们对他人有价值，而一致性是关键。 但是，如果有人对共享整个过程的想法感到不自在，则可以只共享最终产品。

演示很棒，现在是时候开始在社交媒体上建立您的个人品牌了。 让我们开始吧！

然后，他解释了为什么在社交媒体上建立个人品牌是有益的四个简单原因，包括获得必要的工具、能够使用不同的平台、没有竞争以及几乎没有规则可循。 简单工具

要打造个人品牌，您需要具备良好的写作技巧，包括文案写作和技术写作，以吸引人们的注意力并在各种平台上推广您的内容。 在时事通讯、博客和社交媒体等不同平台上重新利用内容也很重要。 写作可以帮助您发现有效的系统，例如从标题开始并将想法总结成要点。 创意的产生也是建立个人品牌的重要组成部分。
建立个人品牌所需的第一个工具是写作技巧。 一开始很难想出点子，但写得越多，就越容易。 讲故事对于建立你的个人品牌很重要，你必须努力不断改进你的写作。 第二个工具是过滤器，可确保您发布能够吸引合适受众的优质内容。 你要考虑你的内容是否有用，是否对他人有益，而不是仅仅为了点赞而做。

建造简单

他将社交媒体平台比作操作系统，并强调了用户在决定关注并与品牌或个人建立联系之前所经历的三步算法。 第一步是给人留下好印象，因为印象与个人资料访问密切相关。 最终目标是鼓励用户访问您的个人资料并建立联系，从而建立您的个人品牌。 有两种方式可以在社交媒体上获得印象——发布内容和与其他人互动。 与已经拥有大量受众的其他人互动可以让您获得更多知名度，尤其是在您刚开始时没有任何关注者的情况下。 这可以通过对讨论做出有意义的贡献并被可能想要查看您的个人资料的其他人注意到来实现。 但是，不建议使用聊天 GPT 增强型插件来回复垃圾邮件，因为这会很烦人并对您的声誉产生负面影响。 人们往往更容易记住消极的经历而不是积极的经历。

当人们看到你的回复时，他可能不会回应，但他会记住你。 是否在社交媒体上关注某人的决定很快发生，通常在查看他的个人资料后几秒钟内。 因此，重要的是要有一个简单明了的个人资料，其中包含关于您的工作、从哪里开始的简短说明以及一个链接。 链接太多可能会让人不知所措，并阻碍人们与您互动。 这是建立社交媒体形象的简单算法的最后一步。

没有竞争

要指出的是，在建立个人品牌时，只要记得在内容中添加个人元素，就没有竞争。 简单地发布大量通用材料而不添加个人风格不会打造成功的个人品牌。 添加独特和个性化的元素，例如分享购买的柴火图片或诸如掷刀或射箭等爱好，将使您的个人品牌脱颖而出并消除竞争。

没有规则

奖金

演示者以一张关于积极社交媒体游戏的奖励幻灯片结束他的演讲，在该游戏中，每个人都共同努力实现更高的目标，没有赢家或输家。 他鼓励分享经验并参与公共运动建设，使比赛更加积极。 感谢您聆听他的介绍。

哪种模式更适合博客获得更多受众，总是选择独特的主题或选择共同的主题 Vaibhav Jain

印度

他个人更喜欢独特的话题，不只专注于技术写作。 他通过解决他已经解决的问题来开始他的文章。 虽然流行的话题可以帮助吸引更多的观众，但独特的话题可能写起来更有成就感。 最终，如果以数量为目标，热门话题可能会更快、更容易地接触到广泛的受众。

有什么技巧或技巧可以增加社交媒体的关注度吗？ 智慧Ovhemerutho-Athe

尼日利亚

相反，他建议专注于创造有价值的内容并关心您的受众以获得长期成功并建立个人品牌。 黑客可能会奏效，但他需要时间和精力，依赖它们并不理想。

分享一些关于如何在您的内容被复制时忽略的提示 Amanpreet Singh

印度

建议是尽快忽略社交媒体上的任何负面或不必要的评论。 考虑长远并建立个人品牌很重要，所以不要陷入小事，例如有人复制您的推文。 最好避免冲动地回应，并从长远来看专注于你的目标。

你能告诉我们这个工具的名称吗？ 我们很乐意使用它们，因为他还有助于提高技能并提高我们的生产力 Omkar Ramishte Ramishte

原文:

Hey there! Did you hear about the Hashnode Bootcamp? It was a three-day event from May 1-3 that helped developers like us improve our writing skills, connect with other professionals, and get ahead in our careers. Even if you're just starting out in the tech world, this Bootcamp was perfect for you!

If you had participated, you would have had so many benefits! You could have made your voice heard in the tech community, met new people to help you in your career, and improved your writing skills. Plus, you would have inspired other developers to do his best too!

The Hashnode Bootcamp was an amazing opportunity to learn new things and be a part of the tech community. You could have gained so much knowledge and made connections that would help you in the future. So, don't miss out on the chance to grow both personally and professionally!

Leveraging Social Media for Monetization by [Nader Dabit] @dabit3

Nader, the director of DevRel at Aave Companies, talks about content creation, personal branding, and social media and how he all fit together. He shares his non-traditional tech background, being a self-taught developer who started coding in 2013 and creating community events in 2014. He started sharing all the things he learned, which led him to write his first blog post in 2015 that received over a million readers, inspiring him to continue creating content. He also started doing conferences in 2016 and started a training company in 2017 called React Native Training, which helped him secure an opportunity to work at AWS for three years before moving into the blockchain space for the last two years.

Personal Branding

Nader talks about the importance of personal branding and how people's non-presence (when he's not physically present) says a lot about them. In a world where people are very busy and have limited attention, it's important to make the most of people's time when he give it to you. This means being clear about what you want to get across on platforms like Twitter and LinkedIn so that you stand out in a sea of generalists. Having a clear biography or description of who you are or who you want to be is important, and it can be helpful to pay specialists to review and recommend updates for your personal online presence.
Nader talks about his experience in a photo shoot that helped them get more opportunities by having a clear and consistent profile picture. He emphasize the importance of specialization in a specific field instead of blending in with everyone else. He advise spending more time than others on a particular technology or skill to become really good at it, which leads to more opportunities and continuous optimization. Nader also suggests linking all social media and online profiles to direct people to the main thing you want them to know about you. It is vital to assume that everyone is time constrained and to be concise and stand out in a job application or online presence.

Online presence / social media

Nader recommends "learning in public" by sharing what you're learning online, including code snippets, GitHub repos, and short videos. This can lead to opportunities and building relationships. It's important to be consistent, helpful without expecting anything in return, and authentic. Being authentic may feel uncomfortable, but it is important for building genuine relationships. To be authentic on social media, you should be yourself and not focus on pleasing everyone else. Engage with influential people in your desired field and interact with them positively. It's important to be consistent on social media and find a balance between engaging and being gimmicky. Use tactics that draw attention without relying too heavily on clickbait. Additionally, consider backlinking your social media profiles on Stack Overflow.

To increase engagement on social media, it's important to link to all of your other socials and use rich media such as videos and images. Be authentic and true to yourself, even if it means going against what your audience wants. Format your writing with proper spacing and headers, and consider collaborating with others to reach a larger audience.

Content creation

Nader emphasized the importance of stepping out of one's comfort zone for successful content creation. Learning something new and condensing it into understandable information presents an opportunity for effective teaching and sharing with others. Nader's process involves learning, building, and using the new knowledge to create content, such as building an app to better understand technical content. Nader discusses his process of open sourcing a project, documenting it in a blog post, creating a tutorial video, and using it for conference talks and workshops. He explain the benefits of reusing content in different formats and creating resources to help others. He encourage content creators to not be intimidated if something has already been done before and to document his own solutions to problems.

Nader advises to not be discouraged if someone else has already created a project or solved a problem, but instead to try and improve upon it or add his own unique touch. He suggest tapping into other networks for scale and specialize in one thing rather than being mediocre at many things. Lastly, he suggest mixing existing ideas to create something new and innovative. The idea of "good artists borrow, but great artists steal" applies to technical content creation.

What are some creative ways for bloggers to monetize his social media presence beyond traditional sponsorships or ads? Suman Giri

100 Devs, Nepal

Consulting is an often overlooked way to monetize your skills. By identifying companies in need of technical content for his DevRel team, you can offer your services on a monthly basis and charge a significant amount, such as $5,000 a month. Many companies have large budgets for this type of service and it can be a big value for them. By offering consulting services, you can make more money, do less work, and build better relationships with clients.

Is ad revenue worth adding?? Since it reduces the quality of the blog.. Please suggest some alternatives for it Devarshi Shimpi

ByteMakers. India

It's not worth adding advertisements unless you're a massive company because the value you get from the traffic is often not much. Instead, you can subcontract as a consultant or contractor for a company looking for DevRel support and earn more money without showing ads. You can still put some content on your own blog.

What resources do you use to effectively manage social media? I have a hard time dealing with that.
Saswat Pal Pal

To be effective on social media, it is important to choose a few platforms to focus on and not attempt to do everything. TikTok and Twitter are great platforms for creating technical content. Once you have created content, you can duplicate it and share it on other platforms like LinkedIn and Twitter. The key is to focus on a few platforms, instead of trying to be active on everything.

Would you please explain with a help of an example that how do you present a content that is presented before with a new touch? Yug Borana

School, India

A developer created an open source project by converting Python code for chat GPT plugins into JavaScript. This led to consulting opportunities, despite not being able to take them due to being busy. The developer notes that there are many tutorials for popular topics, and one can simply present his own view on the subject to gain traction.

How to write good content and what are the points we should always keep in mind while writting Ankita thakur thakur

India

Creating good content involves teaching complex topics in an easy to understand way, condensing information into something concise, and having a high-quality title that engages readers. To achieve this, one should choose a topic he are interested in, make the content digestible for all levels of the audience, and simplify complex topics without being too basic. If these are accomplished, then the content is successful.

Please what's your advice to someone who doesn't like learning in public to leverage technical writing to attract opportunities. I personally get anxiety from public learning but I'm enjoy sharing technical content on social media Stephany Ikebudu

Nigeria

Nader shares technical content as part of his "learning in public" process of experimenting and sharing what he've learned. He believe that everyone creating and sharing content is also learning in public. The main idea is to put things out into the world and make them valuable to others, and consistency is key. However, if someone is uncomfortable with the idea of sharing the whole process, it's okay to just share the end product.

Building Your Personal Brand on Social Media by [Ákos Kőműves]

The presentation was great, and now it's time to start building your personal brand on social media. Ákos offers fresh insights on how he have built his own personal brand and considers a good joke about imposter syndrome. Let's get started!

Ákos introduces himself as a programmer with over 20 years of experience and a former CTO who now freelances and enjoys writing. He then explains four simple reasons why building a personal brand on social media is beneficial, including having access to necessary tools, being able to use different platforms, having no competition, and there being few rules to follow. Ákos notes that the presentation will focus on Twitter, but the advice applies to other platforms as well. Ákos promises to explain any caveats in building a personal brand on social media.

Simple Tools

To build your personal brand, you will need good writing skills, including copywriting and tech writing, to capture people's attention and promote your content on various platforms. Repurposing content is also important across different platforms like newsletters, blogs, and social media. Writing helps you discover efficient systems, such as starting with a title and summarizing ideas into bullet points. Idea generation is also a crucial component of building your personal brand.
The first tool you need to build your personal brand is writing skills. It's hard at first to come up with ideas, but the more you write, the easier it gets. Storytelling is important for building your personal brand, and you have to work on constantly improving your writing. The second tool is a filter to ensure you put out quality content that will attract the right audience. You have to think about whether your content is useful and beneficial to others, rather than just doing it for likes.

Simple to Build

Ákos discusses the simple tools needed to build a personal brand on social media platforms. He compare social media platforms to operating systems and highlight the three-step algorithm users go through before deciding to follow and connect with a brand or individual. The first step is making a good impression, as impressions are closely correlated with profile visits. The ultimate goal is to encourage users to visit your profile and connect, allowing you to build your personal brand. There are two ways to get impressions on social media- posting content and interacting with other people. Engaging with others who already have a big audience can give you more visibility, particularly if you're starting out with no followers. This can be done by making meaningful contributions to discussions and being noticed by others who may want to check out your profile. However, it is not recommended to use chat GPT enhanced addons to spam replies as it can be annoying and have a negative impact on your reputation. People tend to remember negative experiences more than positive ones.

When people see your reply, he may not respond but he will remember you. The decision of whether to follow someone on social media happens quickly, usually within seconds of looking at his profile. Therefore, it's important to have a simple and clear profile with a short statement about what you do, where to start, and a link. Having too many links can be overwhelming and discourage people from engaging with you. This is the final step in a simple algorithm for building your social media presence.

No Compitition

The point being made is that when building a personal brand, there is no competition as long as you remember to add personal elements to your content. Simply posting a lot of generic material without adding personal touches will not make for a successful personal brand. Adding unique and personal elements, such as sharing pictures of firewood purchased or hobbies such as knife throwing or archery, will make your personal brand stand out and eliminate competition.

No Rules

Ákos discusses three things related to social media: reposting of content by others, dealing with gurus who use tactics Ákos doesn't approve of, and the unpredictability of social media algorithms. Ákos advises not engaging with reposted content or getting upset with gurus, and encourages adapting to changes in social media algorithms.

Bonus

The presenter ends his talk with a bonus slide about a positive social media game in which everyone works together to achieve a higher goal without winners or losers. He encourage sharing learnings and being a part of the building in public movement to make the game even more positive. Thank you for listening to his presentation.

Which pattern is better for blog to attain more audience, always choose unique topics or to choose common topics Vaibhav Jain

India

Ákos suggests that the approach to blogging depends on your goals. He personally prefer unique topics and do not focus solely on technical writing. He start his articles by addressing a problem he have solved. While popular topics can help gain more audience, unique topics may be more fulfilling to write about. Ultimately, if quantity is the goal, popular topics may be faster and easier to reach a broad audience.

Is there any trick or hacks to increase social media following? Wisdom Ovhemerutho-Athe

Nigeria

Ákos admits to knowing many hacks and tricks to attract a large audience quickly, but he do not recommend using them. Instead, he suggest focusing on creating valuable content and caring about your audience for long-term success and building a personal brand. The hacks may work, but he take time and effort, and relying on them is not ideal.

Share some tips on how to ignore if your content is copied Amanpreet Singh

India

The advice is to ignore any negative or unnecessary comments on social media as quickly as possible. It's important to think about the long term and building a personal brand, so don't get caught up in small things like someone copying your tweets. It's better to avoid responding impulsively and focus on your goals in the long run.

can you please tell or provide us the names of this tool as well . We would love to use them as he also help in upskill as well as it aslo increasing our productivity Omkar Ramishte Ramishte

na

Ákos tried three tools for scheduling tweets and recommends Typefully as it is the cheapest option and also works for LinkedIn. He suggest checking out the free plan.

As a beginner should we post varieties of stuff like jokes,tech,sports or should be only focused about our niche like only tech and blogging what do you suggest? Nikhil Mishra Mishra

Netaji Subhash Enqineerinq College, India

Ákos encourages personal touches in personal branding. He suggest focusing on one main topic for content and being selective about what is posted, considering if it will benefit the audience in some way. Personal posts can be added for a more personal factor, but should be in a small ratio compared to helpful content. Ákos has a strong filter for what he post and think before posting.

What's the best way to choose a specific niche for your personal brand? (When you're interested in multiple topics) Penaaz Valecha

Scoutfio, India

Ákos wrote a blog post on how to choose a niche. His advice is to consider what is most enjoyable for you to write about, as he personally can't write about things he don't enjoy. This is his top priority when deciding what to write about.

How will you promote your blog post or documentation? Will you use social media, email marketing, or other channels to reach your target audience? Nithish Vaithya

India

Ákos is discussing how social blogging platforms like hash note can do the promotion for you, which is why he are beneficial. However, you can also promote your blog posts through Twitter or by learning about SEO and how Google indexes content. SEO can help you gain long term traffic and solid gains in terms of visits to your blog posts by attracting more readers.

How much time one should to give on writing a quality content Adarsh Rawat

GEHU Dehradun, India

Quality content is not necessarily perfect content, but rather content that serves its purpose and helps, motivates, or entertains others. Time invested in creating quality content should be based on how much time is needed to produce understandable and beneficial content for readers. Focus on creating the next piece of content and don't stress about being perfect.

Wrap Up

Hey guys, Day 2 of the Hashnode Bootcamp on Mastering the Art of Technical Writing is over! We learned a lot from the speakers Nader Dabit @dabit3 and Ákos Kőműves about how to make money and build a personal brand on social media. It was super helpful and I hope you guys learned a lot too!
Also, a big thank you to Dani Passos @danielapassos for being an awesome host and making the session so fun and interactive!

Thank you for reading!
Soumyadeep Mandal @imsampro

如果你参加了，你会得到这么多的好处！ 您本可以在技术社区中发出自己的声音，结识新朋友以帮助您的职业生涯，并提高您的写作技巧。 另外，您也会激励其他开发人员尽力而为！

您本可以获得如此多的知识并建立对您将来有帮助的联系。 所以，不要错过个人和专业成长的机会！

你相信我们可以通过博客 + youtube + 付费课程创建而不是成为一名全职开发人员来过上好日子吗？ 阿米特·塔库尔

阿拉伯联合酋长国

詹姆斯不确定他是否应该被一个问题冒犯，但他说他可以肯定地回答他在他的工作中赚了很多钱。 他愿意透明地讨论他的收入，因为他相信有些人怀疑这是可能的。 例如，他只用一半时间在内容上工作，一年就赚了六位数。 创作者可以通过在 TikTok、YouTube 和 Instagram 等平台上建立大量受众，然后利用这些受众开展业务来赚取大量收入。 通过提供免费价值，然后提供付费产品或服务，创作者可以通过博客、创建在线课程和其他形式的内容创作来谋生。 这样做还提供了最大的灵活性和对一个人工作的控制，以及明确证明一个人能力的能力。

他强调，作为一名全职内容创作者，一切都是他的责任，包括计算税收和物流。 虽然成为全职内容创作者是有可能的，但这需要坚持和实践。 詹姆斯多年来一直这样做，直到他担任现在的职务。

最初更注重质量还是数量来吸引观众？ 印度

创建内容的最大建议是刚开始。 不要想太多或试图让它变得完美，只需发布​​它并将其用作下一个项目的学习经验。 尽早关注数量，然后努力提高每个内容的质量。 通过关注有效的和无效的并不断努力改进来迭代您的过程。

假期结束后，您如何重新开始创作内容？ 这是我开始拖延的时候
苏普里亚

为了在你的工作中保持一致，专注于做让你兴奋的事情，而不是仅仅因为你觉得你必须这样做。 这不仅适用于创建内容，也适用于你的整个职业生涯。 看看你所做的一切，找出你喜欢的方面，然后尝试做更多。 这将使您的体验更加愉快，并将体现在您的工作中。
詹姆斯建议与经理交谈或寻找不同的角色，以花更多时间做您感兴趣的事情。 重要的是要重新评估那些不会让你兴奋的任务，并将你的时间花在做更多你热衷的事情上。 建立例行程序对于生产力和问责制也很重要。 休假后恢复正常生活对于提高工作效率至关重要。 花时间做让您兴奋的事情，并确保他融入您的日常生活。

我该如何处理作家的障碍？ 就特定主题运行系列是否安全？ 乔伊·阿卡诺鲁

詹姆斯承认作家瓶颈的问题，并建议转向其他事情是可以的。 他鼓励个人有意识地考虑他对什么感到兴奋，以帮助克服写作障碍。 这不仅有助于打破思维空间，还可以为内容产生新的想法。 詹姆斯最后强调承认个人需求的重要性，例如散步或编码以克服写作障碍。
詹姆斯建议在回来做一些令人兴奋的事情之前，花点时间让自己做一些能带来快乐的事情。 他还鼓励在已经涵盖的主题上创建内容，因为在找到引起他们共鸣的解决方案之前，总是有人在寻找答案并考虑多种观点。 因此，一个人独特的声音和经验可以为那些寻求答案的人提供价值。

我应该在学习的同时制作内容吗？ 喜欢在刚开始时与人交谈，录制并与人分享吗？ 史瑞亚·米什拉

据一位教过 300 多人的训练营老师说，对于处于职业生涯早期的人来说，最重要的建议是分享他的学习历程。 分享你学到的东西可以让你成为一个更成功的学习者并巩固你的知识，同时也让你有机会练习对面试很重要的沟通技巧。 此外，分享您的学习历程可以作为一个组合来提供可信度并向潜在雇主展示您的知识和经验。
分享您的进步可以让更多人关注您，从而可能带来工作机会。 当您学习新事物时，您是创建该主题内容的最相关人员，因为您清楚地记得学习该事物的感觉。

您如何看待未来技术写作的角色演变？ 沙尚夏尔马

有抱负的软件工程师，印度

技术写作和内容创作角色的重要性与日俱增，尤其是在开发人员体验和营销方面。 有效的文档和内容可以提高公司的转化率和收入。 人工智能的使用可以增强这些角色并使个人能够更有效地工作，但它不会完全取代它们。 可以利用工具和技术的发展，同时仍然确保在最终批准生成的内容时具有个人风格。 总的来说，这些角色的重要性正在增长和认可。

编写清晰简洁的技术文档有哪些最佳实践？ 沙尚夏尔马

有抱负的软件工程师，印度

为了在内容创作中有效地交流想法，重要的是要提前计划并专注于简洁而不是试图用不必要的词来填补空间。 内容的消费者更喜欢更短、更直接的材料，带有标题、要点和强调重要信息的标注。 通过将读者的注意力吸引到相关信息，分解段落和句子进一步提高了可读性。 在内容创作中注重细节可确保最大程度地吸引读者。

他邀请听众提出任何后续问题或参与。 包起来

最后，最后一天的总结，Hashnode 训练营的第 3 天关于将你的专业知识货币化和探索机会，以及一些关于如何通过社交媒体和自由写作赚钱的很酷的技巧。 最后但并非最不重要的一点是，感谢Dani Passos @danielapassos是一位了不起的主持人！ 感谢您让本次会议如此互动和有趣！

感谢您的阅读！

原文:

Hey there! Did you hear about the Hashnode Bootcamp? It was a three-day event from May 1-3 that helped developers like us improve our writing skills, connect with other professionals, and get ahead in our careers. Even if you're just starting out in the tech world, this Bootcamp was perfect for you!

If you had participated, you would have had so many benefits! You could have made your voice heard in the tech community, met new people to help you in your career, and improved your writing skills. Plus, you would have inspired other developers to do his best too!

The Hashnode Bootcamp was an amazing opportunity to learn new things and be a part of the tech community. You could have gained so much knowledge and made connections that would help you in the future. So, don't miss out on the chance to grow both personally and professionally!

Freelance Writing Opportunities for Developers by [James Q Quick] @jamesqquick

James Q Quick is a full-time technical content creator with over 10 years of experience in software development and developer advocacy. He is excited to be at the Hashnode Bootcamp and hopes everyone will get a lot out of it. He creates YouTube videos, spends time on Twitter and TikTok, does courses, public speaking, and consulting for content. He also focuses on intertwining writing as he builds up his brand and repurposes content across different platforms. James is happy to answer any questions.

So what inspired you to start freelance writing as a developer? How did you get in this journey?

James's kick starting point for creating content was a requirement for his job as a technical evangelist, which involved creating demos and speaking at workshops. He initially didn't have any interest in content creation, but eventually saw it as a way to build his brand, get exposure, and make money. He started by writing an article for a platform called scotch.io and eventually began creating blog posts from his YouTube videos to get more exposure and make money. His personal brand building and content creation eventually led him to a full-time career with ultimate flexibility and control.

What types of freelance writing opportunities would you say that are generally available for developers?

Many companies have guest author programs, such as Auth0, Twilio, Scotch IO, and Pot Rocket. These programs allow companies to bring in outside content creators and pay them for his work. It's a great opportunity to get exposure for your brand, build your platform, and get paid for creating content. Additionally, some companies offer sponsored articles and videos as a way to learn more about other products while prioritizing your time. These guest author programs are a fantastic opportunity to expose your brand and get paid while continuing to build your platform.

How important would you say is having a niche and a freelance writing word for developers? How do you choose your niche or is that important and should you write about everything and anything?

How do you go with that?

James discusses the benefits and drawbacks of having a specific niche when creating content. While being known for a specific topic can help establish expertise and attract an audience, it may limit one's options in the future if the topic loses popularity. However, James suggests that having a niche is not necessarily permanent and that he personally maintain a general focus on web development and JavaScript while occasionally niching down for specific projects, such as building a course on a framework called Astro.
James plans to focus on a certain topic for six months to build deeper technical experience and potentially create a paid product at the end. This allows for flexibility in exploring different topics and staying up-to-date with new technologies. The goal is to provide value to the community through content creation.

How can writers market themselves effectively to attract new clients? Amul Gaurav

Cling Multi Solutions, India

To be a successful guest author, it is important to have a portfolio of high-quality articles that demonstrate your writing abilities and expertise in a particular field. These articles should be grammatically correct, technically accurate and reviewed by others to ensure his quality. It is also important to actively engage in the community by sharing code snippets, tips, and responding to other people's tweets. By doing so, your brand exposure increases, and people become more aware of the valuable content you create. Networking is essential, and it is about engaging and showing up for the community, rather than being selfish.

What are some resources or tools that freelance writers can use to improve their productivity and efficiency? Amul Gaurav

Cling Multi Solutions. India

James advises that people should not let the idea of building his own website or blog hold them back. He suggest that using Hashnode is a great place to start and that users can later customize and build his own website and cross-post to Hashnode. James recommends using a plugin called front matter CMS in VS code to manage markdown content. Lastly, he emphasize the importance of tracking ideas and planning as a content creator.
James has many more ideas for content than he can create and tracks them using a running ideas section in Notion. He recommend writing about topics that excite you and using AI tools like Hashnode and Chat GBT for inspiration. He also recommend using Video Tap to transcribe YouTube videos into blog posts. James also mentions the VS code extension Front Matter CMS for managing embedded markdown.

As content creators, can you advise us on the correct way to work with AI technologies and the incorrect way? Thanks, James!
Michael Larocca

James discusses the fear of whether AI will replace developers and content creators. He mention a tool called "Video Tap" that uses AI to write a blog post based on video content, but emphasizes that the creator still needs to approve and edit the post to ensure quality and voice. He suggest using AI for inspiration and making edits to fit the audience's expectations. James also mentions the importance of using AI to suggest impactful titles and metadata to attract readers among the countless content options online.

What do recruiters see in a candidate when hiring for a technical writer? Also, are there a positions in Technical writing like SDE (Junior, midlevel or senior) Shashank Sharma

Aspiring Software engineer, India

There are technical writing positions available within companies, often within a dedicated content team focused on creating blog posts and documentation. These positions can be important for building SEO value and establishing trust and expertise within a given field. Companies without a strong content presence may be viewed with skepticism compared to those with proven knowledge and best practices. Such positions are readily available within many organizations.
James confirms that there are different levels within technical writing, similar to other software engineering roles. The most important skill for a technical writer is being able to communicate technical concepts to audiences of varying levels of experience. The ability to translate technical concepts into understandable language for different audiences is crucial. To showcase his skills, aspiring technical writers can write for themselves, contribute to open-source documentation, and reach out to people already in the job title for advice.

Do you believe we can make good living from blogging + youtube + paid course creation instead of being a full time developer? Amit Thakur Thakur

United Arab Emirates

James is not sure if he should feel offended by a question, but he say he can definitively answer that he make good money in his line of work. He are willing to discuss his income transparently, as he believe some people doubt it's possible. As an example, he made six figures in one year by only working on content for half of it. Creators can make a lot of money by building a large audience on platforms like TikTok, YouTube, and Instagram and then leveraging that audience to build a business. By providing free value and then offering paid products or services, creators can make a good living from blogging, creating online courses, and other forms of content creation. Doing this also provides ultimate flexibility and control over one's work, as well as the ability to definitively prove what one is capable of.

James plans on continuing to build a business around content creation and learning about marketing. He emphasize that as a full-time content creator, everything is his responsibility, including figuring out taxes and logistics. While it is a possibility to become a full-time content creator, it requires consistency and practice. James has been doing it for years leading up to his current position.

Initially focus more on quality or quantity to attract the audience? Vaibhav Jain

India

The biggest recommendation for creating content is to just start. Don't overthink it or try to make it perfect, just publish it and use it as a learning experience for your next project. Focus on quantity early on and then work to increase quality with each piece of content. Iterate on your process by paying attention to what worked and what didn't and continually strive to improve.

How do you get back to creating content after a vacation? This is when the procrastination kicks in for mee
Supriya M

To stay consistent in your work, focus on doing things that excite you rather than just doing things because you feel like you have to. This applies not just to creating content but to your whole career. Look at everything you do and figure out what aspects you enjoy and try to do more of that. This will make the experience more enjoyable for you and it will show in your work.
James suggests having a conversation with a manager or looking for a different role to spend more time doing things you're excited about. It's important to reevaluate tasks that don't excite you and pivot your time to do more of what you're passionate about. Building a routine is also important for productivity and accountability. Getting back into a routine after vacation is essential for productivity. Spend time doing things you're excited about and make sure he're incorporated into your daily routine.

How do I deal with writer's block?? Also is it safe to run a series on a particular topic? Joy Akhanolu

OneSocialCode, Nigeria

James acknowledges the issue of writer's block and suggests that it's okay to pivot to other things. He encourage individuals to intentionally consider what he're excited about to help overcome writer's block. James also shares his personal experience of going through phases of creating content and emphasizes the importance of taking breaks to focus on technical work such as coding. This not only helps break up mental space but also generates fresh ideas for content. James concludes by emphasizing the importance of acknowledging personal needs such as going for walks or coding to overcome writer's block.
James suggests taking time for oneself to do things that bring joy before coming back and doing something exciting. He also encourage creating content on topics that have already been covered because there are always people searching for answers and consuming multiple perspectives before finding the solution that resonates with them. Therefore, one's unique voice and experience can provide value to those searching for answers.

Should I be making content alongside learning? Like talking to people, recording and also sharing it with people when you are just starting? Shreya Mishra

100devs, India

The most important advice for people early in his careers is to share his learning journey, according to a bootcamp teacher who has taught over 300 people. Sharing what you learn makes you a more successful learner and reinforces your knowledge, while also giving you an opportunity to practice communication skills important for interviews. Additionally, sharing your learning journey can serve as a portfolio to provide credibility and showcase your knowledge and experience to potential employers.
The idea of 100 days of code is great because it keeps you engaged and accountable for an extended period of time. Sharing your progress can lead to more people following you, potentially leading to job opportunities. As you learn something new, you are the most relevant person to create content on that topic because you intimately remember what it's like to learn that thing.

How do you see the role of technical writing evolving in the future? Shashank Sharma

Aspiring Software engineer, India

The importance of technical writing and content creation roles is growing, particularly in terms of developer experience and marketing. Effective documentation and content can lead to increased conversion rates and revenue for a company. The use of AI can augment these roles and enable individuals to do more efficiently, but it will not entirely replace them. The evolution of tools and technology can be taken advantage of while still ensuring a personal touch in the final approval of generated content. Overall, these roles are seeing growth and recognition in his importance.

What are some best practices for writing clear and concise technical documentation? Shashank Sharma

Aspiring Software engineer, India

To effectively communicate ideas in content creation, it is important to plan ahead and focus on being concise rather than trying to fill space with unnecessary words. Consumers of content prefer shorter and more direct materials, with headers, bullet points, and call outs emphasizing important information. Breaking up paragraphs and sentences further improves readability by drawing the reader's attention to relevant information. Attention to detail in content creation ensures maximum engagement with readers.

James expressed gratitude for being invited and admires Hashnode and its work. He invite listeners to reach out with any follow-up questions or engagement. James also invites people to join his community on Discord called Learn Build Teach at learnbuildteach.com.

Wrap Up

And Finally, a wrap for the last day, Day 3 of the Hashnode Bootcamp on Monetizing Your Expertise and Exploring Opportunities with some cool tips on how to make money through social media and freelance writing. James Q Quick @jamesqquick , who's a full-time content creator and freelancer, shared some awesome advice with us.
And last but not least, a big shoutout to Dani Passos @danielapassos for being an amazing host! Thanks for making this session so interactive and fun!

Thank you for reading!
Soumyadeep Mandal @imsampro

Arun Kumar Palani - Apr 24

Items.Title: Hashnode 训练营第 1 天：欢迎和开发人员写作介绍 | Hashnode Bootcamp Day 1: Welcome and Introduction to Writing for Developers
Items.Description:
嘿！ 你听说过哈希节点训练营吗？ 这是一个从 5 月 1 日到 3 日为期三天的活动，它帮助像我们这样的开发人员提高了写作技巧，与其他专业人士建立联系，并在我们的职业生涯中取得成功。 即使您刚刚开始涉足科技领域，这个训练营也非常适合您！

如果你参加了，你会得到这么多的好处！ 您本可以在技术社区中发出自己的声音，结识新朋友以帮助您的职业生涯，并提高您的写作技巧。 另外，您也会激励其他开发人员尽力而为！

您本可以获得如此多的知识并建立对您将来有帮助的联系。 所以，不要错过个人和专业成长的机会！

他还在互联网上写作和写博客。 他将讨论为什么写作对软件工程师很重要，并引用他学习不同编程语言和技术的经验，包括在 2014 年从 Java 过渡到 JavaScript。Ankur 谈到作为一个大型分布式团队的一员从头开始从事一个大项目。 沟通具有挑战性，因此他建议建立一个汇合页面。 他看到了利用自己在编写 React 方面的专业知识为该项目做出贡献的机会。 他还对互联网博客文章的写作风格感兴趣，并开始了自己的领域。 有效的沟通在软件开发中是必不可少的，无论是口头的还是书面的。 随着一个人在职业生涯中的进步和承担诸如编写 RFP、团队成员的自我发展和改进计​​划以及记录绩效等任务，写作技巧变得越来越重要。 有效写作对于有效交流有关项目或想法的想法和想法至关重要。 它是仅次于编码的技术行业发展的第二重要技能。 寻求建议和资源来提高你的写作技巧。

他还邀请其他人通过直接消息或加入他的时事通讯来向他们提问，以便更好地了解他的写作过程。

如何在写作中保持一致并获得写作的兴趣 Ankit Singh Mehra

学院, 印度

他建议在强迫自己定期写作和等待灵感来袭之间找到平衡。 他解释说，每个人的方法可能不同，重要的是要试验并找到适合每个人的方法。 他承认设身处地地思考以真正理解写作习惯如何运作的重要性。

获得写作灵感的正确方法是什么 Abimbola Adebayo

他建议使用Hashnode之类的搜索工具寻找灵感，将写作思路积压起来，以备日后参考。 在保持专注和完成一篇文章时，Ankur 建议分配时间并在更小、更易于管理的部分中处理更多技术主题。 他建议把想法写下来，然后再编辑，找到一种独特的写作风格。 他还建议分析他人的写作风格并复制他的思维过程。 他还讨论了进入技术写作生涯的步骤，包括良好的沟通技巧、理解技术堆栈以及可能具备编码知识。 他提到技术写作可以是一个很好的职业，有远程机会和创业世界对作家的高需求。

一篇博文应该有多长？ 我知道它应该取决于主题，但有没有办法确定长度，或者我应该制作一系列关于该主题的博客，详细说明它的所有内容。 希曼舒巴拉尼

个人所得税，印度

关于博客文章的长度，取决于内容和受众。 重要的是要记住注意力跨度很低，因此标题和副标题对于帮助读者浏览内容很重要。 为观众写作很重要，而不仅仅是为自己写作。 就成功的博客文章而言，定义和理解受众是关键。

对于刚开始接触技术并试图通过博客记录他的旅程的人，您有什么建议？ 史瑞亚·米什拉

他建议开始定期撰写有关您正在学习的内容的博客文章可以帮助您提高写作技巧。 然而，写作并不适合所有人，也不应成为压力的原因。 如果你想通过写作赚钱，你需要有效、创新，并且以独特的视角吸引你的听众。 否则，很难脱颖而出并成为杰出的开发人员。

您如何保持最新的技术写作技巧并随时了解该领域的最新趋势和最佳实践？ 幸运西科利亚

自由职业者，肯尼亚

他会花大约 15-20 分钟浏览 Twitter 并关注来自不同行业的人，看看他在做什么并获得新鲜的想法。 他还认为，一旦您精通并付出 100% 的努力，就很容易转向不同的技术。

这是由自由职业技术作家 Swapna Kumar Panda 主持的关于利用技术写作促进开发人员职业发展的讨论。 该会议将涵盖技术写作对开发人员的重要性、如何提高技术写作技能，以及它如何促进职业发展。 它将探讨技术写作的好处和挑战，并展示它如何为开发人员提供机会。 该会议的目的是鼓励开发人员尝试将技术写作作为一种职业选择。

我们为什么要讨论它？

民主的三个支柱（立法、行政、司法）的概念可以与产品码头的三个支柱相关：应用程序、关系和文档。 应用程序是指致力于将产品变为现实的团队，而关系是产品与营销和法律等其他部门之间的接口。 文档是第三个支柱，它对于确保产品的顺利运行至关重要。 没有文档，新的团队成员可能会犯同样的错误，并且不会从过去的经验中吸取教训。 总的来说，文档对于确保生产力和避免重复错误至关重要。

什么是技术写作？

他将技术写作定义为用于各种技术和办公研究领域的交流，但特别关注软件开发。 该行业的技术作家通常为最终用户编写用户指南和手册、API 或软件开发工具包文档、培训材料、工作说明、标准操作程序和白皮书。 技术写作在软件行业中必不可少，以确保清晰地传达和理解复杂的技术概念。
这篇文章讨论了技术作家制作的内容的不同格式，包括文本或 Web 浏览器友好格式，例如博客或带有文本、广告和图像的文章。 作者还指出，图形材料、音频内容、播客和视频内容也可用于创建更具互动性和吸引力的内容，从而与观众建立联系。

对开发人员的重要性

技术写作很重要，因为它允许记录和共享重要的发现和解决方案。 如果没有毕达哥拉斯定理、伽利略的发现、牛顿的运动定律或爱因斯坦的理论的记录，后代就无法从中学习并继续在科学和其他领域取得进步。 技术写作允许知识从一代传到下一代，并允许持续进步。 开发人员是问题解决者，他们在开发应用程序时会遇到许多技术问题。 他通过进行广泛的研究、访问 Stack Overflow 等各种网站并记录他的发现来解决这些问题。 然而，如果他不记录他的解决方案，他可能会忘记他是如何解决这个问题的，并且以后不得不再次重复同样的研究。 记录解决方案不仅可以帮助开发人员记住他的解决方案，还可以帮助其他可能遇到类似问题的人。 他建议创建个人品牌并提供与品牌的订阅或合作机会。 还提供付费内容和代笔机会。 熟练的技术作家可以从改进的沟通技巧和增加的知名度中受益。 还提供技术写作方面的全职工作机会。

如果您通过技术写作赚钱，您的生活方式将会改善。 可能出现的其他机会包括成为一名教育工作者、制作课程、指导人们、公开演讲、成为名人以及成为某些产品或品牌的品牌大使。 这些机会来自通过您的内容与他人分享您的知识和经验。

如何提高你的技能？

首先，重要的是在尝试写之前在特定领域进行一些研究或体验。 其次，必须知道如何以清晰简洁的方式呈现信息，包括有效的标题以及概述问题和子问题。 目标是以一种能够与读者建立联系并为面临类似问题的人提供有用信息的方式进行写作。 对于那些不想阅读整篇文章的人，建议包括整篇文章的摘要。 此外，为目标受众使用有用的视觉效果和正确的语言也很重要。 技术写作需要注意细节以避免拼写和语法错误。 他强调了使用 Hashnode AI 协助编写和改进内容的好处，例如制作好的标题和概述想法。 作为开发人员，平衡技术内容与平易近人的写作风格很重要。

在获得更多机会时保持工作与生活的平衡很重要。 您可以通过学习和撰写新主题（例如 React 或 Node.js）来丰富您的内容，以扩大您的受众并与更多人建立联系。 这样，您可以从一个领域开始，然后扩展到许多其他领域并增加您的追随者。

Leveraging Technical Writing to Land Opportunities and Boost Your Developer Career by [Swapna Kumar Panda]

This is a discussion led by Swapna Kumar Panda, a freelancing technical writer, on leveraging technical writing to boost developer careers. The session will cover the importance of technical writing for developers, how to improve technical writing skills, and how it can lead to career growth. It will explore the benefits and challenges of technical writing, and show how it can provide opportunities for developers. The aim of the session is to encourage developers to try technical writing as a career option.

Why are we discussing it?

The concept of three pillars of democracy (legislative, executive, judiciary) can be related to the three pillars of a product dock: applications, relations, and documents. Applications refer to the team working towards bringing the product to reality, while relations are the interface between the product and other departments such as marketing and legal. Documentation is the third pillar, and it is crucial for ensuring smooth functioning of the product. Without documentation, new team members may make the same mistakes and not learn from past experiences. Overall, documentation is vital for ensuring productivity and avoiding repeated errors.

What is Technical Writing?

Swapna is discussing the importance of technical writing in the software development industry. He define technical writing as communication used in various technical and office research fields, but focus specifically on software development. Technical writers in this industry typically write user guides and manuals for end users, API or software development kit documentation, training materials, statements of work, standard operating procedures, and white papers. Technical writing is essential in the software industry to ensure clear communication and understanding of complex technical concepts.
The passage discusses different formats for content produced by technical writers, including textual or web browser friendly formats such as blogs or articles with text, ads, and images. The writer also notes that graphic materials, audio content, podcasts, and video content can also be used to create more interactive and engaging content that connects with the audience.

Importance to Developers

Technical writing is important because it allows for the documentation and sharing of important discoveries and solutions. Without the documentation of Pythagoras' theorem, Galileo's findings, Newton's laws of motion, or Einstein's theories, future generations would not have been able to learn from them and continue to advance in science and other fields. Technical writing is what allows for the transfer of knowledge from one generation to the next and allows for continued progress. Developers are problem solvers who encounter numerous technical problems while developing applications. He solve these problems by conducting extensive research, visiting various websites such as Stack Overflow, and documenting his findings. However, if he do not document his solutions, he may forget how he solved the problem and have to repeat the same research again in the future. Documenting solutions not only helps developers remember his solutions but also helps others who may encounter similar problems. Swapna discusses the benefits of technical writing and how it can be monetized. He suggest creating a personal brand and offering subscriptions or collaboration opportunities with brands. Paid content and ghostwriting opportunities are also available. Skilled technical writers can benefit from improved communication skills and increased visibility. Full-time job opportunities in technical writing are also available.

If you make money from technical writing, your lifestyle will improve. Other opportunities that can arise include becoming an educator, producing courses, mentoring people, public speaking, becoming a celebrity, and being a brand ambassador for certain products or brands. These opportunities come from sharing your knowledge and experiences with others through your content.

How to improve your skills?

Swapna discusses the importance of having certain basic skills and knowledge for someone starting out in technical writing. Firstly, it is important to have some research or experience in a particular area before attempting to write about it. Secondly, it is essential to know how to present the information in a clear and concise manner, including having an effective title and outlining the problem and sub-problems. The goal is to write in a way that will connect with readers and provide useful information to those facing similar issues. Swapna suggests that when writing an article, it is important to explain the problem and its solutions clearly. It is recommended to include a summary of the entire article for those who don't want to read the entire thing. Additionally, using helpful visuals and correct language for the intended audience is also important. Technical writing requires attention to detail to avoid spelling and grammatical errors. Swapna suggests that it's important to avoid basic language mistakes when writing content and recommends using Hashnode as a platform to publish. He highlight the benefits of using Hashnode AI to assist with writing and improving content, such as producing good titles and outlining ideas. Swapna emphasizes the importance of connecting with the audience and addressing his questions and concerns to maintain a good relationship with them. As a developer, it's important to balance technical content with approachable writing style.

It's important to maintain a work-life balance when taking on additional opportunities. You can diversify your content by learning and writing about new topics, such as React or Node.js, to expand your audience and connect with more people. This way, you can start from one field but expand to many other areas and grow your following.

Success Story

Swapna discussed the importance of technical writing for developers and how it can lead to career opportunities. Swapna then shared his personal success story, beginning as a junior developer in 2006 and progressing to senior developer until having to quit due to health issues. After a difficult five years, Swapna turned to technical writing in 2021, sharing his content on Twitter and blog platforms, which eventually led to his first paid opportunity in 2022. Swapna continues to learn and grow in this field.
The session discussed the importance of documentation and how developers can benefit from technical writing. Technical writing offers career growth opportunities, such as public speaking and becoming a brand ambassador. Swapna invites questions and can be contacted on Twitter.

Wrap Up
